title: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series
display_title: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series
wikitext:
[]: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Nineball » Sat Jun 11, 2016 3:24 pm In the Evangelion fanbase, and by extension anime fanbase it seems like there are two camps in regards to the characters Shinji: A troubled, traumatized youth thrusted into a hostile situation or an easily manipulated emotional trainwreck that nearly doomed the entire world for three people. Asuka: A hot-headed pilot that uses a "Tough Girl" personality as a coping method for her mothers death, or an egotistical, angry tsundere. Gendo: A mentally unstable man who wants to reunite with his dead wife by any means necessary or a horrific cross between Dr Frankenstein and General Ross who wants to end the world and turn everyone into LCL\Matrix Tang just to have bone his likely to be disappointed wife. Rei: A brilliant deconstruction of silent protagonists and "doll" characters who's arguably the most mentally stable character on the show and one that many can empathize with due to her introverted personality or a bland, blank slate of a character that ultimately did more harm than good for the arguement against using "doll" characters, silent protags, generic mc's etc. Kaworu: The only person who showed Shinji any sort of compassion ironically enough happens to be the very thing that he was constantly told to fear (Angel) or UBER-GAY BISHIE SPESS JEBUS. Mari: A great deconstruction\parody of Mary Sues, or just a bad Mary Sue. Evangelion Itself: A brutal deconstruction of Mecha Anime, Harems, and Monster of the Week shows with a deep psychological look into the fractured psyches of what are essentially mad men\women put in charge of a nuclear missile silo and an overall message on how its better to explore the world than just shut it out all together OR a monster of the week show that was supposed to be sci-fi mixed together with light psychological horror that instead turned into a confusing clusterf**k of random religious\sexual imagery that was caused due to death threats, rule 34, a director ready to go postal, and a company that blew it's load so f***ing hard that they nearly went bankrupt. Personally I'm in the middle camp. I like the show and I don't think it's a toilet fire nor do I think the characters are as bland, emo or idiotic as some people say but at the same time I don't think the show is flawless, nor do I like all the characters. (For example even before I found out about what they did in EOE Shinji, Gendo and Rei still occasionally creeped me out for some reason)

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jun 15, 2016 10:32 am Mari tangent went thataway

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Wed Jun 15, 2016 10:39 am Repost- What you presented in your post are people who actually ended up understanding the series vs people that were like "2deep4me". Why is there such polarization? In my opinion I would need to chalk it up to Evangelion being a hit and miss show and the types of people that have watched Eva. From what I have seen, people that have more of the left view tend to be people that can emphasize with Shinji in someway. For people on the right side I feel like they are aren't able to emphasize and chalk it up to calling Shinji a "Whiny little bitch". I'll put that as a short answer, I hope that makes some sense.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 15, 2016 2:28 pm So, there are "two camps", but you're in the middle one, therefore there are actually three, amirite? False dichotomies are false. Thankfully, NGE is a rich enough work that almost none of its characters can be accurately boiled down to shades of black and white.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jun 16, 2016 10:09 am Wait, is this about the TV series or the new movies? Because the polarizations are different for each series. One has a "2DEEP4ME" rage, the other has a "2SHALLOW4ME" rage.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jun 16, 2016 10:15 am Repost of my own: Sicarius VI wrote: Repost- What you presented in your post are people who actually ended up understanding the series vs people that were like "2deep4me".

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by We Are Nice To You » Fri Jul 01, 2016 12:04 am I kinda see this but see it differently. Like, generally these are the kind of attitudes toward I've identified in what conversations about the show I've had. People who dismiss the show and think it is pretentious. Often the show was over hyped for them or explained to them to be something that they saw it wasn't. Sure, fine. People who like it for being a mecha action show with a gritty feel and aesthetic, and typically hate the ending of the TV series because of the character focus. Often say to just watch EoE and skip the TV ending. Sorta overlaps with the first group in some areas. People who basically treat the entirety of Eva as a series of wiki articles and obsess over every single religious reference and piece of technobable. Maintext of the show or anything surrounding the production of the show doesn't matter to them and they'll be upset if you talk about any non-religious/technobable Eva related thing. Only the religious elements and technobable are important and If you don't care about those elements as much as they do or do not prescribe to the exact same fan theories they do, they will claim you never watched the show. This is the typical Eva poster I see on 4chan. Lots of young fans who seem to love the show the most for the characters, finding them very relatable. Often like the TV ending. Lots of these fans I know are queer in some way. Lots of headcanons about Shinji being a trans girl. Critics who appreciate the show but like to talk about Evangelion as conversation starter about other topics since Eva is a popular anime and thus a good reference point.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 02, 2016 4:18 am Bagheera wrote: There's very little polarization on these issues among people who actually understand the show

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jul 02, 2016 5:14 am Reichu wrote: Un-der-stand... the show? What is this "understand" you speak of?

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 02, 2016 5:33 am Reichu wrote: Un-der-stand... the show? What is this "understand" you speak of?

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 02, 2016 6:53 am Reichu wrote: Un-der-stand... the show? What is this "understand" you speak of?

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jul 02, 2016 8:04 am What I had in mind was the necessity of translating idiom as well as words. Concentrating on the words alone can lead to a false idea of actual meaning. But interpretation is another matter altogether, and could apply equally if the show was originated in English.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Sat Jul 02, 2016 9:18 am I feel like Reichu was just trying to make a joke.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jul 02, 2016 11:12 am (As was my response initially)

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 02, 2016 7:27 pm pwhodges wrote: What I had in mind was the necessity of translating idiom as well as words. Concentrating on the words alone can lead to a false idea of actual meaning. But interpretation is another matter altogether, and could apply equally if the show was originated in English.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by akagi » Tue Jul 05, 2016 9:57 am some of it definitely has to do with the way different audiences' cultures affect how they'll think of evangelion. Japanese audiences are far more receptive to timid, "weak" protagonists (like Shinji) who don't fit into the stereotypical mold of a heroic, unemotional male protagonist. american audiences...not so much. i'd guess it also involves how much people enjoy the show and how much time they actually think about it. people who don't like evangelion very much (even if for a reason as simple as 'it just doesn't suit my tastes') are far more likely to not go into in-depth analyses of the characters in evangelion. (why would they?) they're more likely to cast off Asuka as nothing more than a whiny tsundere because they never cared about the character or her development very much in the first place. evangelion isn't really that ~~profound~~ but some of its scenes try to give real depth, albeit in a pretty showy and occasionally pseudo-intellectual manner. many people are put off by this. others aren't, and even like those scenes. these people pay more attention to the characters and are thus more appreciative when their characters arcs turn out to be impressive Japanese audiences are more likely to appreciate evangelion than american ones, but of course there are people in every camp. how much people enjoy the overall show affects how they'll appreciate the characters. evangelion moves too slowly for some people; many episodes have little development and only focus on a character's psyche. these people, annoyed by what they perceive to be tedious attempts to make something not that interesting ~deep~ dislike the characters. and that's not bad--liking evangelion definitely doesn't make someone intelligent or cultured, just as disliking it doesn't mean you're some buffoon who can't appreciate complexity. the ways evangelion tries to bring complexity, however, can go unnoticed or be seen as pretentious and annoying

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Director Black » Tue Jul 05, 2016 10:01 pm To me, it mostly has to do with how people view it with their mindset. Many of the people who like/love Eva are ones who have walked in the character's shoes. Someone who hasn't gone through many of these things just can't relate. And before you say anything, yes, there are many people who will no doubt be turned off by the avant-garde approach of the way it gets it's message across. However, the important thing to realize for those people is that even if they don't like Eva, you can show them where the acclaim and fans come from. It's understandable for someone to not like Eva, but it's important to understand why this avant-garde anime with a complete mind fuck of a movie (Be it an amazing one) got this big with people to begin with.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by NONAME » Thu Jul 07, 2016 11:06 pm From what I have read, some people only liked the show because of the action and the sci-fi and post-apocalyptic stuff, they didn't care about the psychological and philosophical development. When I finished the show and read some opinions, I was surprised that a lot of people were complaining about Asuka and Shinji personalities (especially Shinji) and people saying that the end is pretentious and skippable. I even read a guy who said that every time an 'emo' monologue came, he just skipped it totally, lol. And I don't understand, what's the point of seeing an entire show if you hate the main characters and you don't like the development at all? I think that those people just want to see cool characters doing cool things (I think that this is te mainly reason why Rei is so loved) instead of more realistic or humans characters. Then NGE is definitely not their anime. And there is also the people who think "yeah is good but not as good as everyone says" and that's OK, I think that can happen to everyone with another show/music/movie/book . I think that the people who can relate to the characters are more likely to be "hooked" and love the series. I understand that, to the normal people I guess it's only a good show with a little bit too much of sadness.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Director Black » Fri Jul 08, 2016 8:50 pm NONAME wrote: I think that the people who can relate to the characters are more likely to be "hooked" and love the series. I understand that, to the normal people I guess it's only a good show with a little bit too much of sadness.

[]: Re: Why Is There so much Polarization Regarding the Series?             Quote Post by Xxx_Generic Name_Xxx » Wed Jul 13, 2016 11:53 am Because to apreciate the series you must be in the characters shoes,but if you have a happy-go-lucky life,you will hate the show because you din'the experienced the characters pain.
